title: olearia megalophylla
intro: Olearia megalophylla, also known as tree daisy bush, is a shrub native to Australia with large, lobed leaves and white or blue flowers. It is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever and inflammation, as well as aiding in wound healing and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, Olearia megalophylla is often made into a tea or tincture and used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and skin conditions like eczema and acne.
